title: adonis flammea subsp. polypetala
intro: Adonis flammea subsp. polypetala, also known as the red dragon or mountain adonis, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations of Adonis flammea subsp. polypetala can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
